notes: Lesson 18 Orientation (act 103), 'Two Pointer — the family map', 903.91s / ~15.1min, 7 long cues spread over the real overview audio (following the proven orientation pattern of acts 7/14/38/72/76/80/98). Cue00 the O(n^2) pair search (nested i/j loops, 'pairs tried' counter, O(n^2)); Cue01 sorted makes a comparison decisive (1+11=12>10, the logical leap 'no pair with 11 works', cross off the whole right end, one-compare-discards-one-candidate => O(n)); Cue02 the converging branch (full zig-zag 12>/9</11>/9< to 4+6=10 found at 2,3); Cue03 the read/write branch (compact [1,1,2,3,3] in place, read scans / write marks the kept prefix, read n + write<=n = O(n) O(1) space); Cue04 the fast/slow branch (a flat linked list, slow +1 fast +2, gap readout, fast hits the end -> slow at the middle, plus cycle detection); Cue05 when it fails (an UNSORTED row [8,3,11,1,6] where 'too big -> drop right' is a lie, 11 hides in the middle, force-the-pattern gives a fast WRONG answer); Cue06 recognition & window kinship (trigger phrases + two shapes converging/same-direction + 'a sliding window IS a same-direction two-pointer — same family'). arrays.py idioms; MathTex reserved for complexity bounds. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke each cue, full -ql render (7 chunks 854x480x15, niced/serial), READ start/mid/end for every cue. Iteration 2 fixed three defects and re-rendered the affected cues: Cue01 target chip overlapping the last value cell (moved upper-right); Cue03 two bottom captions ('jobs' + 'principle') colliding at the bottom edge (added FadeOut(jobs)); Cue05 the top 'converging pointers assume a sorted row' note overlapping the 'but this row is UNSORTED' tag (raised the note to y=2.4, lowered the row, moved the target chip to the lower-right). Re-verified all three clean. The Cue04_end and Cue05_end frames show the NEXT cue's title — the documented chunk-pin keyframe-seek boundary artifact, not a content defect; those cues' settled content is verified via their mid frames and (for Cue06's kinship line, which lands after the mid) an accurate-seek extraction at 866s. Cue06 has no _end frame (last cue near-EOF). Per-cue checklist (legibility + math/algorithm correctness, highlight matches the beat, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 903.801s vs audio 903.91s (delta 0.109s, within tolerance).
